comments.body:
> [!NOTE] > ## Reviews paused > > It looks like this branch is under active development. To avoid overwhelming you with review comments due to an influx of new commits, CodeRabbit has automatically paused this review. You can configure this behavior by changing the `reviews.auto_review.auto_pause_after_reviewed_commits` setting. > > Use the following commands to manage reviews: > - `@coderabbitai resume` to resume automatic reviews. > - `@coderabbitai review` to trigger a single review. > > Use the checkboxes below for quick actions: > - [ ] ▶️ Resume reviews > - [ ] 🔍 Trigger review No actionable comments were generated in the recent review. 🎉
ℹ️ Recent review info
⚙️ Run configuration **Configuration used**: defaults **Review profile**: CHILL **Plan**: Pro Plus **Run ID**: `9bc38ce4-31e2-4a28-90df-09766c0114a6`
📥 Commits Reviewing files that changed from the base of the PR and between 475e69e7e3c6e08c4057cef13c19524de47e1909 and 8e59aeb37acd0786a112eeabfad74357591290b1.
📒 Files selected for processing (2) * `packages/client/src/lib/__tests__/shapeIndexLifecycle.test.ts` * `packages/client/src/lib/__tests__/trackInterpolation.test.ts`
🚧 Files skipped from review as they are similar to previous changes (1) * packages/client/src/lib/__tests__/trackInterpolation.test.ts
---
📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough The PR adds a per-Mode in-memory cache for routes, stops, and last-train payloads, memoizes shape indexes by RoutesGeoJSON reference, wires cache hydration/persistence into useRouteShapes and useTrainPositions, changes aircraft/train lifecycle handling on mode flips, and adds tests validating cache and prewarm behavior. ## Changes **Mode-Aware State Caching and Lifecycle Optimization** | Layer / File(s) | Summary | |---|---| | **Cache Module Contract**
`packages/client/src/lib/modeCache.ts` | Introduces module-level `Map`-based caches for routes, stops, and trains (with `fetchedAt` timestamp), keyed by `Mode`. Exports getter/setter pairs for each dataset and a `clearMode(mode)` function to purge a mode's cached entries. | | **Shape Index Memoization**
`packages/client/src/lib/trackInterpolation.ts` | `buildShapeIndex` memoizes by `RoutesGeoJSON` object via a `WeakMap` and uses a module-level `shapeIdCounter`; on cache hit returns the cached index and schedules prewarm only for new indexes. Adds `_resetTrackCachesForTests()` to clear runtime prewarm caches for tests and simplifies idle scheduling. | | **useRouteShapes Cache Integration**
`packages/client/src/hooks/useRouteShapes.ts` | Integrates `getRoutes`/`setRoutes`/`getStops`/`setStops`; implements cache-first hydration on mount with synchronous stops enrichment, then conditionally fetches and caches only missing datasets. Clears state early on `mode === null`. | | **useTrainPositions Cache Integration**
`packages/client/src/hooks/useTrainPositions.ts` | Integrates `getLastTrains`/`setLastTrains`. On mode change, hydrates `data` and `lastUpdated` from cache instead of clearing immediately (unless mode is `null` or no cached data exists). On each successful poll, persists result to cache before updating React state. | | **Aircraft Hook & TransitMap**
`packages/client/src/hooks/useAircraftFeatures.ts`, `packages/client/src/components/Map/TransitMap.tsx` | `useAircraftFeatures` gains an `enabled: boolean` parameter and clears internal aircraft state when disabled; TransitMap passes `mode === null` to the hook to drop aircraft when leaving airspace view. | | **useTrainFeatures Reset**
`packages/client/src/hooks/useTrainFeatures.ts` | Adds a mode-keyed effect that resets interpolation refs and lastRenderedFraction on mode changes to prevent cross-mode interpolation leakage. | | **Cache Module Tests**
`packages/client/src/lib/__tests__/modeCache.test.ts` | Vitest suite verifying undefined initial state, reference preservation on set/get, per-mode isolation (subway/LIRR), `fetchedAt` timestamp wrapping for trains, same-mode overwrite behavior, and `clearMode` isolation. | | **Shape Index Lifecycle Tests**
`packages/client/src/lib/__tests__/shapeIndexLifecycle.test.ts` | Vitest suite with `makeRoutes` helper and `setTimeout` stubbing for jsdom. Verifies `buildShapeIndex` memoization returns same instance on identical routes reference and returns new instance on different routes payload; resets track caches between tests. | | **Track Interpolation Test Adjustments**
`packages/client/src/lib/__tests__/trackInterpolation.test.ts` | Tests now import and call `_resetTrackCachesForTests()` in `beforeEach` to ensure isolation after buildShapeIndex no longer clears snap/bestShape caches; replaces prewarm cancellation expectation with a test asserting two independent prewarms run. | ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 4 (Complex) | ⏱️ ~45 minutes ## Possibly related PRs - [cjunks94/panoptrain#31](https://github.com/cjunks94/panoptrain/pull/31): Related changes to useRouteShapes and routeShapesLoading flow used by TransitMap/MapLoadingBadge. - [cjunks94/panoptrain#30](https://github.com/cjunks94/panoptrain/pull/30): Overlapping changes to trackInterpolation prewarm/caching and buildShapeIndex behavior. - [cjunks94/panoptrain#37](https://github.com/cjunks94/panoptrain/pull/37): Related aircraft-smoothing/airspace hook work that this PR extends with an enabled flag and immediate-clear behavior. ## Poem > 🐰 I cached routes beneath the moonlit tracks, > > Tabs flip quick — no fetching brings me back, > > Shape indexes nap when routes remain the same, > > Trains pop back in like friends who learned my name, > > A hopping cache — swift, small, and full of vim.
🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 4 | ❌ 1 ### ❌ Failed checks (1 warning) | Check name | Status | Explanation | Resolution | | :----------------: | :--------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Docstring Coverage | ⚠️ Warning | Docstring coverage is 25.00% which is insufficient. The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. |
✅ Passed checks (4 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :------------------------: | :------- | :----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Description Check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped - CodeRabbit’s high-level summary is enabled. | | Title check | ✅ Passed | The title 'perf(ui): cache routes/stops + last-train poll across tab switches' clearly describes the primary performance optimization introduced: caching mode-specific data across tab/mode switches. | | Linked Issues check | ✅ Passed | The PR implements the red-phase TDD setup for all objectives from `#53`: new test files verify modeCache behavior and buildShapeIndex memoization; hooks are wired to use cache getters/setters; trackInterpolation supports memoization; all acceptance criteria are addressed. | | Out of Scope Changes check | ✅ Passed | All changes are directly aligned with `#53` objectives: modeCache module and tests, hook integration with cache, buildShapeIndex memoization, aircraft/train state cleanup on mode change, and track interpolation cache management. |
✏️ Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.
✨ Finishing Touches
📝 Generate docstrings - [ ] Create stacked PR - [ ] Commit on current branch
🧪 Generate unit tests (beta) - [ ] Create PR with unit tests - [ ] Commit unit tests in branch `perf/cache-mode-routes-and-trains`
--- Comment `@coderabbitai help` to get the list of available commands and usage tips.

reviews.body:
**Actionable comments posted: 2**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In `@packages/client/src/hooks/useTrainFeatures.ts`:
- Around line 80-96: The mode-change effect currently resets
shapeIndexRef.current = {}, which can race with the route-shape build effect and
wipe a freshly built index causing processSlice (which checks
Object.keys(index).length === 0) to exit early; remove the line that clears
shapeIndexRef from the useEffect that runs on [mode] (or only clear
shapeIndexRef inside the route-shape build effect before it reconstructs the
index) so the routeShapes builder owns resetting/setting shapeIndexRef and
processSlice sees the built index intact.

In `@packages/client/src/lib/trackInterpolation.ts`:
- Around line 93-110: buildShapeIndex currently cancels a global idle prewarm
which can prevent the first mode's prewarm from ever running when routes are
switched; change the cancellation to be scoped per routes payload/index so each
cached entry gets its own scheduled prewarm (or re-schedules when returning a
cached index that hasn't been prewarmed). Specifically, associate the
scheduleIdle handle with the index stored in indexByRoutes (or a companion map
keyed by the RoutesGeoJSON) and call scheduleIdle(() =>
prewarmTrackCaches(index)) when creating or when returning a cached index that
lacks a pending/ran prewarm; stop using a global cancel tied to shapeIdCounter
and ensure prewarm / cancellation logic references buildShapeIndex,
scheduleIdle, prewarmTrackCaches, indexByRoutes, and shapeIdCounter
appropriately.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>🪄 Autofix (Beta)</summary>

Fix all unresolved CodeRabbit comments on this PR:

- [ ] <!-- {"checkboxId": "ff5b1114-7d8c-49e6-8ac1-43f82af23a33"} --> Create a new PR with the fixes

</details>

```
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In `@packages/client/src/lib/trackInterpolation.ts`:
- Around line 94-110: When indexByRoutes misses and you build a new index (the
block creating index and calling scheduleIdle/ prewarmTrackCaches), invalidate
the global bestShapeCache so cachedBestShape entries can't point at stale
ShapeData; specifically, after computing index (or when cached is falsy) call
bestShapeCache.clear() (or remove entries for the affected routeId/grid cells)
before setting indexByRoutes.set(routes, index), or alternatively include a
per-index discriminator (e.g., routes object identity or a generated indexId) in
the keys used by cachedBestShape/findTrackPath so bestShapeCache keys
incorporate the new index; update the code paths around indexByRoutes,
bestShapeCache, findTrackPath, and prewarmTrackCaches accordingly.
```
